Stub functions for clock-related operations in the Pebble firmware tests.

# Purpose
This header file provides a set of weakly defined functions related to time and date manipulation. The functions are intended to be used for formatting and calculating time-related data. Each function is marked with the `WEAK` attribute, which allows them to be overridden by other implementations if needed. This is useful in scenarios where the default behavior needs to be customized.

The functions include [`clock_get_since_time`](<#clock_get_since_time>), [`clock_get_until_time`](<#clock_get_until_time>), and [`clock_get_until_time_without_fulltime`](<#clock_get_until_time_without_fulltime>), which are used to format time differences. The [`clock_hour_and_minute_add`](<#clock_hour_and_minute_add>) function adjusts hours and minutes by a specified number of minutes. The [`clock_copy_time_string_timestamp`](<#clock_copy_time_string_timestamp>), [`clock_get_friendly_date`](<#clock_get_friendly_date>), [`clock_get_time_number`](<#clock_get_time_number>), and [`clock_get_time_word`](<#clock_get_time_word>) functions are used to convert timestamps into different string formats or numerical representations. The file includes headers `services/common/clock.h` and `util/attributes.h`, indicating dependencies on common clock services and attribute utilities.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `services/common/clock.h`
- `util/attributes.h`

### clock\_get\_until\_time<!-- {{#callable:clock_get_until_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_clock.h#L24>)

Provides a placeholder for a function that formats a time until a specified timestamp with a maximum relative hour limit.
- **Inputs**:

    - ``timestamp``: A `time_t` value representing the target time to format until.
    - ``max_relative_hrs``: An integer specifying the maximum number of hours relative to the current time to consider for formatting.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - The function is defined as `WEAK`, indicating it can be overridden by other implementations.
    - The function currently has an empty body, meaning it does not perform any operations or logic.
- **Output**: No output is produced as the function body is empty.

### clock\_hour\_and\_minute\_add<!-- {{#callable:clock_hour_and_minute_add}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_clock.h#L30>)

Adds a specified number of minutes to a given hour and minute.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``hour``: A pointer to an integer representing the hour to which minutes will be added.
    - ``minute``: A pointer to an integer representing the minute to which minutes will be added.
    - ``delta_minutes``: An integer representing the number of minutes to add to the given hour and minute.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - The function is defined as `WEAK`, indicating it can be overridden by other definitions with the same name.
    - The function takes pointers to `hour` and `minute` and an integer `delta_minutes` as parameters.
    - The function body is empty, so no operations are performed on the inputs.
- **Output**: There is no output as the function body is empty and does not modify the inputs.

### clock\_get\_friendly\_date<!-- {{#callable:clock_get_friendly_date}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_clock.h#L36>)

- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - The function is defined as `WEAK`, indicating it can be overridden by other implementations.

    - The function body is empty, indicating it is a placeholder or intended to be overridden.
